ceo.name: Julian Riester / Florian DuBois
ceo.note: GfS operates as a brand within the Assa Abloy Group — the world's largest lock and security hardware manufacturer (founded 1994, HQ Stockholm)

revenue.value: Part of Assa Abloy Group (SEK 140B / ~€12.5B group revenue, 2024)
revenue.note: GfS is a specialized brand within Assa Abloy's EMEIA division; standalone revenue not publicly disclosed

employees.note: Assa Abloy Group total; GfS Rettungswegtechnik is a small specialized unit within the group

company.rdIntensity: Not disclosed (GfS level); Assa Abloy Group spends ~3% of revenue on R&D (~€375M)

sites: 70+ countries worldwide via Assa Abloy distribution

divisions.name: Emergency Exit Solutions
divisions.description: Core GfS product line: Exit Control (Türwächter) door guardians, e-Bar push bars, DEXCON monitoring devices, Day Alarm systems. Emergency exit security with optional wireless monitoring.

divisions.name: Wireless Signal Transmission
divisions.description: EasyWave 868 MHz wireless receivers, repeaters, and BUS interfaces for centralizing door guardian status into building management systems.

divisions.name: Accessories & Mounting
divisions.description: Mounting plates for glass-frame doors, profile half-cylinder keys, external signal devices (strobes, sirens), battery monitoring modules.

productGroup.name: Emergency Exit Door Alarm Guards
productGroup.scope: Door-mounted emergency exit security devices with alarm and optional wireless monitoring — lever handle (EN 179) and panic bar (EN 1125) variants

families.name: Exit Control 179 (Lever Handle)
families.technology: Mechanical guard + piezo alarm, DIN EN 179 compliant
families.status: Serial product — basic, pre-alarm, and pre-alarm + radio variants

families.name: Exit Control 1125 (Panic Bar)
families.technology: Mechanical guard + piezo alarm, DIN EN 1125 compliant
families.status: Serial product — basic and pre-alarm variants

families.name: GfS e-Bar
families.technology: Push bar with integrated alarm mechanism

product.name: GfS Türwächter IoT (Exit Control 179/1125)
product.family: Emergency Exit Door Alarm Guards with Wireless Monitoring

IoT variant — subject of this report

q1.german: Welche neuen Märkte gibt es für den Türwächter IoT, wie attraktiv sind diese Segmente und welche sollten priorisiert werden?
q1.english: What new markets exist for the Türwächter IoT emergency exit guardian, how attractive are these segments, and which should be prioritized?
q1.answer: Ten target markets were identified through NAICS discovery anchored to the commodity functional promise ('prevent unauthorized use of emergency exit doors'). All ten land in the 'investigate' band (5.0–7.5 composite score). Hotels & Motels rank #1 (6.95) due to customer-facing VN position, highest incumbent fragmentation, and cleanest constraint profile. Continuing Care Retirement Communities rank #2 (6.80) driven by highest ODI opportunity and elopement liability ROI. Elementary & Secondary Schools rank #3 (6.61) with the strongest feature-market fit. No market reaches 'pursue' (>7.5) without pilot validation, and no market is eliminated (zero knockouts across all constraint screens).

q2.german: Wo verursacht eine fehlende oder unzuverlässige Überwachung von Notausgängen die höchsten Kosten und Risiken?
q2.english: Where does missing or unreliable emergency exit monitoring cause the highest costs and risks?
q2.answer: The highest-leverage markets are Continuing Care Retirement (elopement liability, CMS citations), Hospitals (Joint Commission egress violations, patient safety), and Schools (post-Uvalde legislation, lockdown compliance). In each case, the dominant cost driver is regulatory non-compliance (fines, license risk, litigation) rather than the direct cost of the hardware. Hotels face brand/liability exposure from unauthorized exit use. The IoT overlay's value proposition is strongest where manual patrol is most expensive (large distributed facilities) and where automated compliance documentation reduces audit labor.

portfolioPriorities.hardwareDelta: Standard product + BUS gateway for multi-property chains

High opportunity — strongest ODI, elopement liability ROI

portfolioPriorities.role: Volume play — 100K+ schools, best feature-market fit

Standard product — surface-mount is the differentiator
portfolioPriorities.role: Niche — heritage building constraints favor retrofit design

financials.note: Financial scenarios are not part of the new_markets workflow scope. Per project instructions, BMC/GTM analysis will be conducted separately. Pilot investment ranges are per-market estimates from the ranking analysis.
